statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{k-5}+\frac{3}{k+5}}}{{1-\frac{1}{k+5}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{k-5}+\frac{3}{k+5}} and {1-\frac{1}{k+5}}, which is {\left(k-5\right)\mathopen{}\left(k+5\right)}.
Since k + 5 was canceled, k \ne -5 is a lost domain condition.
Although k - 5 was also canceled, k \ne 5 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
